task: Research quiet luxury web design, write v3 spec for squad landing page
what_worked: Looking at real reference sites (Linear, Stripe, Vercel) gave concrete design language
Framing critique as 'what feels old-fashioned and why' made spec actionable
Connecting design philosophy to specific component changes (not just vague direction)
what_failed: v2 spec was already detailed but missed the 'taste' dimension — functional completeness != design quality
lesson: Design specs need a 'taste layer' — not just what to build, but what feeling to achieve. Reference real sites as north stars, not just describe abstract principles.
user_feedback: Xiaosong said v2 looked 'old-fashioned' — the spec was technically solid but didn't capture modern design sensibility
